articles.summary: Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks. articles.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]

articles.summary: Moscow’s agents are building fake companies, recruiting middlemen and deploying cyber spies and hackers who gather information that could be used to attack key infrastructure. articles.title: Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
articles.summary: The one-click vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on self-hosted Flowise servers by tricking users into importing a malicious chatflow. The post Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
articles.summary: A newly discovered local privilege escalation vulnerability dubbed 'CIFSwitch' in the Linux kernel could allow attackers to forge CIFS authentication key descriptions, abuse the kernel's key request mechanism, and gain root privileges. [...]

articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. articles.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
articles.summary: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to display fake OpenAI outage pages that direct users to download malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. [...]

articles.title: California AG sues 23andMe over 2023 breach exposing health data
articles.summary: California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, now Chrome Holding Co., over the company's failure to protect sensitive customer genetic and personal information. [...]

articles.title: ChatGPhish Vulnerability Turns ChatGPT Web Summaries Into a Phishing Surface
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in OpenAI ChatGPT that leverages the artificial intelligence (AI) assistant's implicit trust in Markdown links and images to trigger prompt injections and open the door to phishing attacks. articles.title: MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform
articles.summary: MokN's platform deploys realistic decoy access points to lure attackers into revealing compromised credentials, enabling organizations to respond before abuse occurs. The post MokN Raises $15 Million for Phish-Back Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: From $5 Attacks to Botnet-Powered Platforms: Inside the DDoS-as-a- Service Market

articles.title: Dutch govt disrupts malware botnet with 17 million infected devices
articles.summary: Dutch authorities have taken offline a massive botnet of 17 million devices and seized more than 200 servers at a local provider that supported the operation. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft calls zero-day releases ‘never justifiable’ as researcher threatens to drop more
articles.summary: Each vulnerability was published with working proof-of-concept code to the Microsoft-owned code repository GitHub, making them immediately available to both attackers and security professionals.

articles.title: Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: The critical-severity issue, assigned a CVSS score of 9.4, is an argument injection flaw that can be exploited by authenticated attackers via pull requests with malicious branch names. The post Gogs Zero-Day Exposes Servers to Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
articles.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

articles.summary: A likely Russian threat cluster tracked as GreyVibe has been targeting Ukrainian entities with AI-generated lures and a rich set of custom malware tools. [...]

articles.summary: Hackers are exploiting an authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) to deliver an undocumented credential stealer called EKZ. [...]

articles.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions. The security flaw, per Rapid7, is rated 9.4 on the CVSS scoring system. It does not have a CVE identifier

articles.title: How SIEM helps MSPs reduce noise and stop threats faster
articles.summary: MSPs don't lack security data. They struggle to separate real threats from alert noise. Kaseya explains how SIEM helps MSPs improve visibility, reduce fatigue, and respond faster. [...]

articles.title: Cruise giant Carnival confirms data breach affecting nearly 6 million people
articles.summary: The company said the threat actor gained access to a limited portion of its IT environment last month after compromising an employee account. By the end of April, Carnival determined that the attacker had copied personal information from its systems.

articles.summary: Microsoft has come out strongly in favor of Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD), urging the research community to share their findings and give affected vendors an opportunity to better understand the impact and address them before they are publicly disclosed. articles.title: Russia conducting daily attacks on UK 'from seabed to cyberspace,' spy chief warns
articles.summary: Anne Keast-Butler, director of GCHQ, said Russia's actions have prompted the agency to defend subsea cables and energy pipelines in British waters, disrupt Russian networks smuggling sanctioned technology and countering “reckless sabotage and assassination attempts.”

articles.title: Romanian gets 5 years in prison for hacking Oregon govt network
